perturbation.original_text: 2. Confidential Informatio n. “Confidential Information” means any information , including investigator’s brochures, case report forms and protocols, disclosed by or on behalf of Disclosure identified as confidential when first disclosed and provided in tangible form, or if disclosed orally summarized in a writing pro vided by the Discloser to Institution within twenty (20) days after oral disclosure, other than information that: (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by In stitution; (b) is already known by or in the possessio n of Institution at the time of disclosure by Discloser; (c) is indepen dently developed by In stitution witho ut use of or refere nce to Discl oser’s Confidential Information; or (d) is obtained by Institution from a third party that has not breached any obligations of confident iality.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Confidential Informatio n. “Confidential Information” means any information , including investigator’s brochures, case report forms and protocols, disclosed by or on behalf of Disclosure identified as confidential when first disclosed and provided in tangible form, or if disclosed orally summarized in a writing pro vided by the Discloser to Institution within twenty (20) days after oral disclosure. For the avoidance of doubt, any information will be considered as Confidential Information, even if: (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by In stitution; (b) is already known by or in the possessio n of Institution at the time of disclosure by Discloser; (c) is indepen dently developed by In stitution witho ut use of or refere nce to Discl oser’s Confidential Information; or (d) is obtained by Institution from a third party that has not breached any obligations of confident iality.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original definition of "Confidential Information" by removing the exceptions and including information that would otherwise be excluded from the definition, such as information already publicly available or independently developed. This means that clause 2 now states that Confidential Information includes information that is or becomes generally available to the public which is contradictory to what follows. This directly affects section 3. Maintenance of Confidentiality, as the scope of what must be protected is now dramatically broader.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Maintenance of Confidentiality 3.1 Use. Institution agrees to u se the Confidential Information only for evaluation purposes to determine whether to conduct clinical studies sponsored by Discloser . Institution shall not use the Confidential Information for its own b enefit or the benefit of another. and the Regents of the University of Michigan (“Institution”) having business offices at 3003 S. State St., 1000 Wolverine Tower, Ann Arbor, MI 48109-1274. Version 2.1 Last Revised Date: 8/2/16 Approved by: Last Revised By: C. Colthorp 3.3 Care. Institution shall protect Discloser’s Confidential Information using not less than the same care it uses with respect to its own confidential information, but at all times at least reasonable care. 3.4 Required Disclosure. Institution may disclose the Confidential Information to the extent that such disclosure is required by law or court order, provided, however, that Institution promptly provides to Discl oser p rior written not ice of suc h disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' by removing exceptions (a)-(d), now including information previously excluded (e.g., publicly available information). This directly contradicts section 3, 'Maintenance of Confidentiality,' as the scope of what the Institution must protect is significantly broadened. The original text had exceptions to what was considered confidential, whereas the changed text includes information that was originally an exception. This creates a clear and direct conflict, impacting the enforceability of section 3.

This makes the obligations unclear. This contradiction makes clause 3.1 contradictory to the first original sentence on clause 3.1

Institution may use the Confidential Information for its own b enefit or the benefit of another. and the Regents of the University of Michigan (“Institution”) having business offices at 3003 S. Institution may disclose the Confidential Information to the extent that such disclosure is required by law or court order, provided, however, that Institution promptly provides to Discl oser p rior written not ice of suc h disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 3.1 replaces 'Institution shall not use the Confidential Information for its own benefit or the benefit of another' with 'Institution may use the Confidential Information for its own benefit or the benefit of another.' This creates a direct contradiction within the same section (3.1) and the contradicted text is also the modified text, as indicated in the problem description and perturbation explanation. The original text prohibits a specific action, while the changed text permits it, leading to an unclear and unenforceable obligation.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Term and Continuing Obligations 5.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date, and continue for a perio d of one (1) year, or until earlier terminated by one party providing written notice of termination to the othe r, whichever comes first. 5.2 Survival . Institution's duty to protect Discloser’s Confidential Information shall survive th e termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years. 5.3 Return or Destroy. Institution shall promptly return o r, at Discloser’s option, destr oy, all Discloser’s Confidential Information related to a clinical study if Institution does not participate in such clinical study, upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, or upon written req uest by th e Discl oser, provided however, that Institution may retain in confidence under this Agreement one archived copy of the Discloser’s Confidential Information solely for the purpose of administering In stitution’s obligations hereun der.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Term and Continuing Obligations 5.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date, and continue for a perio d of one (1) year, or until earlier terminated by one party providing written notice of termination to the othe r, whichever comes first. 5.2 Survival . Institution's duty to protect Discloser’s Confidential Information shall survive th e termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of zero (0) years. 5.3 Return or Destroy. Institution shall promptly return o r, at Discloser’s option, destr oy, all Discloser’s Confidential Information related to a clinical study if Institution does not participate in such clinical study, upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, or upon written req uest by th e Discl oser, provided however, that Institution may retain in confidence under this Agreement one archived copy of the Discloser’s Confidential Information solely for the purpose of administering In stitution’s obligations hereun der.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the survival period from five years to zero years creates an in-text contradiction with the obligations outlined in Section 5.3, particularly concerning the return or destruction of confidential information after termination or expiration. While 5.3 states that Institution shall return or destroy Discloser’s Confidential Information, the fact that 5.2 indicates that this agreement will not survive after the termination makes 5.3 contratictory as the termination will remove its legal effects.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Term and Continuing Obligations 5.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date, and continue for a perio d of one (1) year, or until earlier terminated by one party providing written notice of termination to the othe r, whichever comes first. 5.2 Survival . Institution's duty to protect Discloser’s Confidential Information shall survive th e termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of zero (0) years. 5.3 Return or Destroy. Institution shall promptly return o r, at Discloser’s option, destr oy, all Discloser’s Confidential Information related to a clinical study if Institution does not participate in such clinical study, upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, or upon written req uest by th e Discl oser, provided however, that Institution may retain in confidence under this Agreement one archived copy of the Discloser’s Confidential Information solely for the purpose of administering In stitution’s obligations hereun der.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 5.2 to state that the Institution's duty to protect Discloser’s Confidential Information survives for a period of zero years. This directly contradicts section 5.3, which outlines the obligations of the Institution to return or destroy the Discloser's Confidential Information upon termination or expiration of the agreement, including retaining a copy for administering obligations. If the agreement does not survive termination as stated in 5.2, then the obligations outlined in 5.3 cannot be enforced. This is a clear in-text contradiction as it creates confusion and renders section 5.3 unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 5.3 Return or Destroy. Institution shall promptly return o r, at Discloser’s option, destr oy, all Discloser’s Confidential Information related to a clinical study if Institution does not participate in such clinical study, upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, or upon written req uest by th e Discl oser, provided however, that Institution may retain in confidence under this Agreement one archived copy of the Discloser’s Confidential Information solely for the purpose of administering In stitution’s obligations hereun der.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.3 Return or Destroy. Institution shall promptly return o r, at Discloser’s option, destr oy, some of Discloser’s Confidential Information related to a clinical study if Institution does not participate in such clinical study, upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, or upon written req uest by th e Discl oser, provided however, that Institution may retain in confidence under this Agreement one archived copy of the Discloser’s Confidential Information solely for the purpose of administering In stitution’s obligations hereun der.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "all Discloser’s Confidential Information" to "some of Discloser’s Confidential Information" creates ambiguity regarding the extent of the return or destruction obligation. It contradicts the original intent of ensuring complete removal or return of all confidential information, opening the door to disputes about what constitutes 'some' and what can be rightfully retained beyond the archived copy. By stating it will only return "some" of Discloser's Confidential Information it creates an In-text contradiction within its own statement, since the last half states that the only instance of retaining information would be the exception of the archived copy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Term and Continuing Obligations 5.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date, and continue for a perio d of one (1) year, or until earlier terminated by one party providing written notice of termination to the othe r, whichever comes first. 5.2 Survival . Institution's duty to protect Discloser’s Confidential Information shall survive th e termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years. 5.3 Return or Destroy. Institution shall promptly return o r, at Discloser’s option, destr oy, some of Discloser’s Confidential Information related to a clinical study if Institution does not participate in such clinical study, upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, or upon written req uest by th e Discl oser, provided however, that Institution may retain in confidence under this Agreement one archived copy of the Discloser’s Confidential Information solely for the purpose of administering In stitution’s obligations hereun der.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 5.3 to state that the Institution shall return or destroy "some" of Discloser's Confidential Information. The original text stated the Institution would return or destroy "all" of the confidential information. The last part of 5.3 (the contradicted text), which has not changed, states the Institution may retain in confidence under this Agreement one archived copy of the Discloser’s Confidential Information solely for the purpose of administering Institution’s obligations hereunder. Therefore, the change from 'all' to 'some' creates a contradiction because if the only exception for retaining confidential information is for the archived copy, then the institution should return or destroy all information *except* the archived copy, not *some* information arbitrarily, thus the change created a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Miscellaneous 7.1 No Obligation. This Agreement does not obligate the parties to enter into negotiations or any subsequent agreement. Any agreement concerning a relationship can only be made in a definitive written agreement, executed on behalf of each party by an authorized representative . 7.2 Entire Agreement. This Agreement is the entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject matter hereof and supersedes a ll prior agreements between the parties relating to the subject matter hereof. No agreement modifying or waiving any provision of this Agreement shall be binding unless made in a writing that references this Agreement and is signed by the part ies. Facsimile or electronic signed copies shall have the same effect as originals.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Miscellaneous 7.1 No Obligation. This Agreement does obligate the parties to enter into negotiations or any subsequent agreement. Any agreement concerning a relationship can only be made in a definitive written agreement, executed on behalf of each party by an authorized representative . 7.2 Entire Agreement. This Agreement is the entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject matter hereof and supersedes a ll prior agreements between the parties relating to the subject matter hereof. No agreement modifying or waiving any provision of this Agreement shall be binding unless made in a writing that references this Agreement and is signed by the part ies. Facsimile or electronic signed copies shall have the same effect as originals.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "does not obligate" to "does obligate" directly contradicts the intent of clause 7.1. The clause is designed to ensure that this NDA does not force either party into future contracts. Now, it states the opposite, which means the two parties are obligated to enter into negotiation which contradicts the statement after that, which establishes further agreements in a document outside this NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Miscellaneous 7.1 No Obligation. This Agreement does obligate the parties to enter into negotiations or any subsequent agreement. Any agreement concerning a relationship can only be made in a definitive written agreement, executed on behalf of each party by an authorized representative . 7.2 Entire Agreement. This Agreement is the entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject matter hereof and supersedes a ll prior agreements between the parties relating to the subject matter hereof. No agreement modifying or waiving any provision of this Agreement shall be binding unless made in a writing that references this Agreement and is signed by the part ies. Facsimile or electronic signed copies shall have the same effect as originals.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement does not obligate the parties to enter into negotiations. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that the agreement does obligate the parties to enter into negotiations. The Contradicted Text is the Changed Text, and because it is the same, the contradiction is within the text itself. The change from 'does not obligate' to 'does obligate' fundamentally alters the nature of the agreement and creates a direct, irreconcilable conflict within the document, fitting the definition of an in-text contradiction.
